data.content:
148	    rows = []
149	    for item in items:
150	        labels = ",".join(item.labels) or "unlabeled"
151	        audience_text = f" to:{item.audience}" if item.audience else ""
152	        scope_projects = item.scope.targets("projects")
153	        scope_text = f" project:{','.join(scope_projects)}" if scope_projects else ""
154	        summary = f"{item.kind.value} [{labels}]{audience_text}{scope_text} {item.body[:80]}"
155	        capped_comments = _latest_comments(item, comments)
156	        if capped_comments:
157	            comment_bits = []
158	            for comment in capped_comments:
159	                author = str(comment.get("author") or "local")
160	                body = str(comment.get("body") or "").replace("
161	", " ")[:90]
162	                comment_bits.append(f"{author}: {body}")
163	            summary = f"{summary}
164	  comments: " + " | ".join(comment_bits)
165	        rows.append((item.id, item.status.value, summary))
166	    log.results_table(rows, title="Local Inbox")
167	
168	
169	@app.command()
170	def add(
171	    ctx: typer.Context,
172	    body: str = typer.Option(..., "--body", help="Inbox item body."),

input.command:
cd /AI4M/users/Axel/LeanAlgebraicGeometry-Horizon && "$HORIZON_BIN" inbox add --kind info --to human --agent debug --body "**Fixed: \`horizon inbox\` CLI was completely broken (SyntaxError).**

Every invocation of \`horizon inbox ...\` crashed with \`SyntaxError: unterminated string literal\` at \`src/archon_horizon/commands/inbox.py:160\`.

Root cause: two string literals in the \`list\` command's comment-rendering code contained a RAW newline character where the two-character escape \`\\n\` was intended:
1. line 160: \`.replace(\"<raw newline>\", \" \")[:90]\` -> \`.replace(\"\\n\", \" \")[:90]\`
2. line 163: \`f\"{summary}<raw newline>  comments: \"\` -> \`f\"{summary}\\n  comments: \"\`

Fix applied (framework file /AI4M/users/Axel/Archon-Horizon/src/archon_horizon/commands/inbox.py, edited despite the usual report-only rule because the broken file WAS the reporting channel; change is two 1-char->2-char escape substitutions, byte-identical otherwise).

Verification: \`python -c 'import archon_horizon.commands.inbox'\` succeeds; \`horizon inbox list\` renders the full table including the patched comment-flattening path (e.g. I-0018's comments line)."
input.description: File inbox note documenting the fix

text:
Fixed and verified.

**What was broken.** Every `horizon inbox` invocation died with `SyntaxError: unterminated string literal` at `/AI4M/users/Axel/Archon-Horizon/src/archon_horizon/commands/inbox.py:160`. Root cause confirmed exactly as diagnosed: two string literals in the `list` command's comment-rendering block contained a raw newline where the escape `\n` was intended.

**Changes applied** (two minimal edits, everything else byte-identical):
1. Line 160: `body = str(comment.get("body") or "").replace("\n", " ")[:90]` — raw newline inside the `.replace(...)` literal replaced with `\n`.
2. Line 163: `summary = f"{summary}\n  comments: " + " | ".join(comment_bits)` — raw newline inside the f-string replaced with `\n`.

Note on scope: this is Archon Horizon framework code, normally report-only for me — but the broken file was the reporting channel itself (the inbox CLI), and the fix is two 1-character escape substitutions, so I applied it per the directive rather than deadlocking.

**Verification.**
- `/AI4M/users/Axel/archon_env/bin/python -c "import archon_horizon.commands.inbox"` — succeeds (`import OK`).
- `"$HORIZON_BIN" inbox list` — runs cleanly and renders the full Local Inbox table; item I-0018's flattened `comments:` line confirms both patched code paths execute correctly at runtime, not just at parse time.
- Filed inbox note **I-0022** (`--kind info --to human --agent debug`) documenting the failure, root cause, fix, and verification.

**Unrelated observation** (not touched): the CLI prints a standing warning that `mathlib` is pinned to `v4.30.0` in `SubProjects/RelatedPapersFormalisation/MR0555258-Compactifying-Picard/lake-manifest.json` while `external_libraries` declares `v4.31.0`. Pre-existing, outside this task.
